name: Joe Marsala
abstract: Joseph Francis Marsala (January 4, 1907 – March 4, 1978) was an Italian-American jazz clarinetist, alto and tenor saxophonist, and songwriter, older brother of trumpeter Marty Marsala.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The fourth of April, I recorded with Joe Marsala and his Delta Four, a session that was supervised by Leonard Feather.

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: he kind of took me under his wing and started giving me clarinet lessons
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Joe Marsala in the context of Buddy Rich's early career ('Buddy Rich came up in that old school way back there with Joe Marsala...'). The interviewee (Cozy Cole) does not respond to this specific mention or claim any personal connection to Joe Marsala.

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Marsala, he gave Zutty some money and told Zutty he wanted him to play the jam session on Sunday afternoon. And that was Zutty's first gig in New York... He paid Zutty thirty-five dollars

about_quotes.speaker: Gordon Lorraine
about_quotes.evidence: I remember there was a clarinet player there, a very handsome guy with a big scar down his face, Joe...Joe...Joe...it will come to me... I know it when I hear it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gordon Lorraine — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Polcer
about_quotes.evidence: He was our original clarinetist and we had Bobby Gordon a very fine I would say Joe Marsala type, Pee Wee Russell influenced clarinetist
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Polcer interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998
